concepts.title: Panchsheel Principles
concepts.description: The Panchsheel principles are the five principles of peaceful coexistence that India and China agreed upon in 1954. These principles are: (1) mutual respect for each other's territorial integrity and sovereignty, (2) non-aggression, (3) non-interference in each other's internal affairs, (4) equality and mutual benefit, and (5) peaceful coexistence.

concepts.title: NAM and India's Role
concepts.description: According to the NCERT textbook, India played a significant role in the Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) and was one of its founding members. NAM was formed during the Cold War era, and its primary objective was to provide an alternative to the bipolar world order dominated by the US and the USSR.

concepts.title: India's Relations with Neighbours
concepts.description: The CBSE board often asks questions about India's relations with its neighbouring countries, particularly Pakistan, China, and Bangladesh. Students should be well-versed in the historical context, current issues, and diplomatic efforts between India and these countries.

concepts.title: SAARC Member Countries
concepts.description: To remember the member countries of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC), use the mnemonic 'AFBGIMNP': Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka.

concepts.title: Confusing ASEAN and SAARC
concepts.description: Students often confuse the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) with SAARC. To avoid this mistake, remember that ASEAN is a regional organization comprising 10 Southeast Asian countries, whereas SAARC is a regional organization comprising 8 South Asian countries.

concepts.title: Key International Organizations
concepts.description: Students should be familiar with key international organizations such as the United Nations (UN), European Union (EU), Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN), and the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC). Remember their founding years, member countries, and primary objectives.

concepts.title: Indo-US Relations
concepts.description: The Indo-US relations have undergone significant transformations since the end of the Cold War. The US has emerged as one of India's closest strategic partners, with cooperation in areas such as defence, trade, and counter-terrorism. Students should be aware of the key developments and agreements between the two countries, including the Indo-US nuclear deal.

concepts.title: India's Foreign Policy Objectives
concepts.description: According to the NCERT textbook, India's foreign policy objectives include protecting the country's sovereignty and territorial integrity, promoting economic development, and ensuring energy security. Students should be able to explain these objectives and provide examples of how they are achieved through India's external relations.
